commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=911241f3ca02a3452a6fa46b13844a2707d29902)
commands.review_summary:
The branch threads optional `toolCallId` through Control UI stream segment types, adjusts chat item sorting to keep a matching live tool card after its stream segment, adds a focused regression test, and removes an unused Telegram rich-message parameter.

PR surface: Source +40, Tests +24. Total +64 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, but not by a live browser run here: current main snapshots stream segments with `Date.now()` and then sorts those segments against tool event timestamps, so an older tool timestamp can move the tool before its causal assistant text.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or human follow-up because the code fix is narrow, but the external-PR proof gate still requires real WebChat/Gateway evidence automation cannot supply for the contributor.

target.title: fix(ui): preserve live tool stream order
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes live WebChat ordering when a tool event timestamp is older than the assistant stream segment that introduced that tool call.
- Threads optional `toolCallId` metadata through chat stream segment types so the renderer can keep the matching tool card after its causal assistant text while preserving ordinary timestamp ordering.
- Adds a regression test for the delayed/live timestamp race behind #92122.

Fixes #92122

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat live streaming can render a tool card before the assistant text that triggered it when the tool message timestamp sorts earlier than the stream segment.

Real environment tested: Local Control UI rendering/model unit tests in the OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 via repo test wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts` with a regression case where `streamSegments[0].toolCallId` matches a tool card whose timestamp is older than the segment.

Evidence after fix: The regression asserts the rendered chat items remain `[assistant stream segment, matching tool card]` even when the tool card timestamp is earlier. I also ran the production Control UI chat-item projection directly after the patch:

```json
{
  "behavior": "live Control UI chat item projection",
  "scenario": "tool timestamp is older than the assistant stream segment that introduced it",
  "visibleOrder": [
    "stream:I will inspect the file.",
    "tool:call-read"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The live projection output shows the assistant stream segment before the matching tool card, and focused chat item tests passed: 34 tests in `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`.

What was not tested: I did not run a full live Gateway session or produce a video artifact. The broader DOM chat render and controller tests passed locally, and `chat-responsive.browser.test.ts` is skipped in this local environment.
target.author: pick-cat

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
